validator.message: Please provide a @FIELD@.
The @FIELD@ must be at most 255 characters.
The @FIELD@ must not contain backslashes or start or end with whitespace.
The @FIELD@ must be at most 255 characters.
The @FIELD@ must be empty or must not contain backslashes or start or end with whitespace.
The @FIELD@ is too long (maximum 4096 characters).
Enter the @FIELD@ as comma-separated non-empty values without backslashes or spaces immediately before or after commas.
Please provide the @FIELD@.
The @FIELD@ is too long (maximum 4096 characters).
Enter the @FIELD@ as comma-separated non-empty values without backslashes or spaces immediately before or after commas.
Backslashes are not allowed in this field.
The @FIELD@ must be at most 8 characters.
The @FIELD@ must be empty or contain 1-8 non-whitespace characters; commas, quotes, and backslashes are not allowed.
Please provide the @FIELD@.
The @FIELD@ must contain 1-8 characters.
The @FIELD@ must contain 1-8 non-whitespace characters; commas, quotes, and backslashes are not allowed.

xml.name: validator_interaction_pairs

validator.message: Provide at least one ligand,receptor pair.
The interaction list is too long (maximum 65535 characters).
Use exactly one ligand,receptor pair per non-empty line, with one comma, no surrounding whitespace, and no backslashes.

xml.validator: "\\" not in value and all(line.count(',') == 1 and all(part and part == part.strip() for part in line.split(',')) for line in value.splitlines() if line.strip())

validator.message: The URL must be at most 2048 characters.
Enter an HTTP or HTTPS URL without whitespace or backslashes, or leave the field empty.

xml.name: inputs_anndata_or_mudata
xml.token_help: AnnData or MuData input. The selected method determines the required datatype.

param.label: Group By
param.help: Key to be used for grouping

param.label: Ligand-receptor database
param.help: Built-in database of ligand-receptor pair annotations

param.label: Return all interactions
param.help: If enabled: include all L-R pairs from database even if they fail expression filter. If disabled: return only pairs meeting expr_prop threshold.

xml.name: param_key_added
xml.token_help: Key in adata.uns where results will be stored.

param.label: Use adata.raw
param.help: Use the expression matrix stored in adata.raw when available. Disable this option to use adata.X or a selected layer. LIANA default: enabled.

param.label: AnnData layer
param.help: Optional key in adata.layers. When selecting a layer, disable 'Use adata.raw'. Leave empty to use the default expression matrix.

xml.name: param_obs_keys
xml.token_help: Comma-separated observation columns to preserve in the output.

xml.name: param_lr_sep
xml.token_help: Separator used to join ligand and receptor names.

xml.name: param_view_sep
xml.token_help: Separator inserted between a view name and its feature names.

xml.name: section_mudata_xy_options
xml.token_help: Used only when the input datatype is MuData (.h5mu).

param.label: X modality
param.help: MuData modality containing source or ligand features.

param.label: Y modality
param.help: MuData modality containing target or receptor features.

param.label: X feature label
param.help: Optional source-feature column label. Empty uses LIANA's default.

param.label: Y feature label
param.help: Optional target-feature column label. Empty uses LIANA's default.

param.label: Differential expression test
param.help: Statistical method for rank_genes_groups (1-vs-Rest). t-test: parametric; wilcoxon: non-parametric, robust; logreg: logistic regression. Default: t-test.

param.label: Permutation test iterations
param.help: Number of random permutations for statistical significance (CellPhoneDB, etc.). Default: 1000. Set to 0 to skip permutation testing.

param.label: Log transformation base
param.help: Base for reversing log-transformation of expression values. Default: e (2.718). Use 2 for log2 or 10 for log10 data.

param.label: Additional columns to be added
param.help: From any of the methods implemented in liana, or columns returned by scanpy, each starting with ligand_* or receptor_*. Enter a comma-separated list, for example ligand_pvals,receptor_pvals.

param.label: Minimum expression proportion
param.help: Minimum expression proportion for the ligands and receptors (+ their subunits) in the corresponding cell identities. Set to 0 to return unfiltered results

param.label: Minimum cells
param.help: Minimum number of cells per cell identity to be considered for downstream analysis. Default: 5.

param.label: Spatial coordinates key
param.help: The key in adata.obsm where the spatial coordinates are stored

xml.name: param_cell_types
xml.token_help: Optional comma-separated subset of cell types. Empty uses all available cell types.

xml.name: param_complex_sep
xml.token_help: Separator used for multi-subunit complexes. Leave empty to disable complex handling.

param.label: Set the diagonal of the connectivity matrix to 1
Radial basis function kernel
param.help: Used for the generation of the connectivity matrix for the paraview

param.label: MISTy prediction model
param.help: Statistical model for feature importance ranking

param.name: resource_cached
param.label: Ligand-receptor database (cached)
param.help: Select from resources installed by the LIANA Resources Data Manager.

validator.message: No cached LIANA ligand-receptor resources available. Install them via Admin → Data Managers → Download LIANA Resources.

param.name: hcop_cached
param.label: Cached HCOP ortholog mapping
param.help: Select an HCOP table installed by an administrator through the LIANA Resources Data Manager.

validator.message: No cached HCOP resources are available. Ask a Galaxy administrator to install and run the LIANA Resources Data Manager.

param.label: Override download URL
param.help: Leave empty to use LIANA's HGNC Google Cloud URL for the selected organism.

param.name: resource_type
param.label: Interaction source
param.help: Priority: interactions list > custom file > built-in database. Choose based on whether you have custom L-R pairs or want to use pre-defined resources.

param.name: resource_source_selector
param.label: Resource source
param.help: Use cached resources from this Galaxy server (installed via Data Manager) for fast offline access, or download from LIANA API.
param.option: Cached on Galaxy server (from Data Manager)

param.label: Custom resource file (TSV)
param.help: Tab-separated file with at least two columns named 'ligand' and 'receptor'. Each row represents one ligand-receptor interaction. This resource will override the built-in database selection.

param.label: Ligand-receptor pairs (one per line)
param.help: Enter specific interactions to analyze in format: ligand,receptor (one pair per line, e.g., 'TGFB1,TGFBR1' or 'IL1A,IL1R1'). Use the same gene symbols as in your expression matrix. This list will override any built-in or custom resource file.

param.label: Sample key
param.help: Key in adata.obs for grouping by sample or condition context. Default: 'sample'.

param.label: Score key
param.help: Column name of the score in liana_res. If empty, inferred from method.

param.label: Source key
param.help: Column name of sender/source cell types in LIANA results. Default: 'source'.

param.label: Target key
param.help: Column name of receiver/target cell types in LIANA results. Default: 'target'.

param.label: Ligand key
param.help: Column name of ligand in LIANA results. Default: 'ligand_complex'.

param.label: Receptor key
param.help: Column name of receptor in LIANA results. Default: 'receptor_complex'.

param.label: Results key in adata.uns
param.help: Must match key_added from inference method

param.label: Color by column
param.help: Column name in liana_res DataFrame to map to dot color. E.g., 'magnitude_rank', 'specificity_rank', 'lr_means', or pvalue columns.

param.label: Size by column
param.help: Column name in liana_res DataFrame to map to dot size. Larger values = larger dots.

param.label: Function to use to sort the dataframe
Source (sender) cell types
param.help: Comma-separated list of source cell type labels. Empty = show all. Useful for focusing on specific signaling populations.

param.label: Target (receiver) cell types
param.help: Comma-separated list of target cell type labels. Empty = show all. Useful for focusing on specific receiving populations.

param.label: Ligand genes
param.help: Comma-separated ligand gene symbols to include. Empty = all genes. Example: IL6,TNF,TGFB1.

param.label: Receptor genes
param.help: Comma-separated receptor gene symbols to include. Empty = all genes. Example: EGFR,FGFR1,PDGFRA.

param.label: Top N interactions
param.help: Number of top-ranked interactions to display.

param.label: Sort column
param.help: Column in LIANA results to sort by (used with top_n).

param.label: Sort ascending
param.help: Sort by increasing (smallest to largest) values.

param.label: Sort by absolute value
param.help: Sort by absolute value magnitude (ignoring sign).

param.label: Apply -log10 transformation to color
param.help: If enabled: apply -log10 to color column (e.g., converts p-values to -log10(p)). Useful for p-value visualization.

param.label: Apply -log10 transformation to size
param.help: If enabled: apply -log10 to size column (useful for p-values where smaller p = larger dots).

param.label: Color scheme (matplotlib colormap)
param.help: Colormap for color column visualization. viridis/plasma: perceptually uniform; RdBu: diverging; sequential: Reds, Blues, etc. Default: viridis.

param.name: size_range_min
param.label: Minimum dot size (pixels)
param.help: Smallest dot size for minimum values. Default: 2 pixels.

param.name: size_range_max
param.label: Maximum dot size (pixels)
param.help: Largest dot size for maximum values. Default: 9 pixels. Adjust range to make size differences more/less prominent.

param.name: figure_size_width
param.label: Figure width (inches)
param.help: Output plot width. Default: 8 inches.

param.name: figure_size_height
param.label: Figure height (inches)
param.help: Output plot height. Default: 6 inches.

macros.token: #if str($method.source_labels) != '': source_labels=[x.strip() for x in '$method.source_labels'.split(',')], #end if #if str($method.target_labels) != '': target_labels=[x.strip() for x in '$method.target_labels'.split(',')], #end if

#if str($method.ligand_complex) != '': ligand_complex=[x.strip() for x in '$method.ligand_complex'.split(',')], #end if #if str($method.receptor_complex) != '': receptor_complex=[x.strip() for x in '$method.receptor_complex'.split(',')], #end if

#if str($method.top_n) != '': top_n=$method.top_n, #end if #if str($method.orderby) != '': orderby='$method.orderby', #end if orderby_ascending=$method.orderby_ascending, orderby_absolute=$method.orderby_absolute,
#if str($method.supp_columns) != '': supp_columns=[x.strip() for x in '$method.supp_columns'.split(',') if x.strip()], #end if

xml.name: version_command
xml.version_command: python -c "import liana as li;print('liana version: %s' % li.__version__)"

macros.token: #if str($method.resource_selection.resource_type) == 'interactions': interactions=[tuple(x.strip().split(',')) for x in '''$method.resource_selection.interactions'''.strip().split('\n') if x.strip()], #else if str($method.resource_selection.resource_type) == 'resource': resource=pd.read_csv('$method.resource_selection.resource', sep='\t'), #else: #if str($method.resource_selection.resource_api_cached.resource_source_selector) == 'cached': resource=pd.read_csv(os.path.join('$method.resource_selection.resource_api_cached.resource_cached.fields.path', '$method.resource_selection.resource_api_cached.resource_cached.fields.value' + '.tsv'), sep='\t'), #else: resource_name='$method.resource_selection.resource_api_cached.resource_name', #end if #end if

ln -s '$adata' 'input_anndata.h5ad' && cat '$script_file' > '$hidden_output' && python '$script_file' >> '$hidden_output' && ls . >> '$hidden_output' && touch 'anndata_info.txt' && cat 'anndata_info.txt' @CMD_PRETTIFY_STDOUT@

import liana as li import scanpy as sc import pandas as pd import numpy as np import os
N_JOBS = int(os.environ.get('GALAXY_SLOTS') or 1)
adata.write_h5ad('anndata.h5ad', compression='gzip') with open('anndata_info.txt','w', encoding='utf-8') as ainfo: print(adata, file=ainfo)
